scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE DISTRICT COURT / CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT WITH COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. This Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as the agreement was entered into at {{PLACE_OF_CONTRACT}}, was to be performed at {{PLACE_OF_PERFORMANCE}}, and the breach occurred at {{PLACE_OF_BREACH}}; in any event, the Defendant resides or carries on business within the local limits of this Court at Bengaluru.

2. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

LIMITATION

3. The suit is within limitation under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date on which the contract was broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

4. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff entered into an Agreement to Sell with the Defendant for the purchase of residential Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, for a total consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/-. The terms of the agreement included the execution of the sale deed upon payment of the balance consideration on or before 10.07.2024. A copy of the Agreement to Sell is annexed hereto as Annexure A.

5. On the date of the agreement, i.e., 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant via RTGS. The transaction bears UTR No. HDFC20240110987654. The Plaintiff holds the receipt for the said payment as proof of part performance of the contract. A copy of the RTGS receipt is annexed hereto as Annexure B.

6. The remaining balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was agreed to be paid by the Plaintiff on or before 10.07.2024 against the execution and registration of the sale deed. The Plaintiff has kept the said balance amount ready and available for payment to the Defendant as per the terms of the agreement.

7. The Plaintiff has always been ready and willing to perform her part of the contract since the date of the agreement. The Plaintiff possesses the necessary funds to discharge the balance liability and has consistently communicated her willingness to proceed with the transaction within the stipulated timeline.

8. The Plaintiff holds the original Agreement to Sell and the payment receipts in her custody. These documents substantiate the existence of the contract, the payment of part consideration, and the Plaintiff's commitment to complete the transaction.

BREACH OF CONTRACT AND PARTICULARS

9. On 05.06.2024, prior to the date fixed for execution of the sale deed, the Defendant sent a letter to the Plaintiff expressly refusing to execute the sale deed. The Defendant cited the receipt of a higher offer from a third party as the reason for his refusal to honor the agreement entered into with the Plaintiff. A copy of the Defendant's refusal letter is annexed hereto as Annexure D.

10. On 20.06.2024, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice to the Defendant calling upon him to execute the sale deed as per the terms of the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024. The notice demanded specific performance of the contract and warned of legal action in the event of continued refusal. A copy of the legal notice along with the postal receipt is annexed hereto as Annexure C.

11. The Defendant failed to comply with the legal notice and maintained his refusal to execute the sale deed. The Defendant did not appear for the registration of the sale deed on the agreed date of 10.07.2024 despite the Plaintiff's readiness to pay the balance consideration.

12. The Defendant's actions constitute a clear breach of the contractual obligations agreed upon on 10.01.2024. By refusing to execute the sale deed and retaining the advance payment without fulfilling the transfer of title, the Defendant has failed to perform his part of the contract.

LOSS AND DAMAGE

13. Due to the Defendant's breach of contract, the Plaintiff has suffered financial loss and deprivation of the benefit of the bargain. The Plaintiff was compelled to incur expenses in issuing the legal notice and pursuing the present litigation to enforce her rights under the agreement.

14. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation for the loss suffered due to the Defendant's failure to perform. The Plaintiff claims damages quantified at Rs. {{CLAIMED_DAMAGES_AMOUNT}}/- towards the loss incurred due to the breach of the agreement and market fluctuation.

15. The Plaintiff is also entitled to the costs of this suit incurred due to the Defendant's wrongful refusal to perform the contract. The Plaintiff seeks recovery of litigation costs as part of the relief granted by this Hon'ble Court.

LEGAL BASIS

16. The Plaintiff is entitled to a decree of specific performance of the agreement under Section 10 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, the subject matter being such that the grant of compensation in money would not afford adequate relief.

17. The Plaintiff has been ready and willing to perform her part of the agreement at all material times within the meaning of Section 16(c) of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

CAUSE OF ACTION

18. The cause of action first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant committed default under the agreement and thereby caused the Plaintiff's right to sue for specific performance to accrue.

19. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's claim for specific performance subsists and remains enforceable in law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

20. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to {{COURT_FEE_COMPUTATION_BASIS}} and the applicable rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Direct the Defendant to execute and register the sale deed in respect of the suit property in favour of the Plaintiff upon receipt of the balance consideration of Rs. {{BALANCE_CONSIDERATION}}/-;
    (b) Direct the Defendant to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the suit property to the Plaintiff (if not already in possession);
    (c) In the alternative, award compensation under Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 in lieu of or in addition to specific performance, at such amount as this Hon'ble Court deems fit;
    (d) Award pendente lite and future interest on the amount paid under Order XX Rule 11 CPC and Section 34 CPC respectively;
    (e) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;
    (f) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper;

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt for advance payment (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)

Annexure C — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

Annexure D — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 20 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowled

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE Commercial Court (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT Bengaluru

Commercial Suit No. __________ / 20____

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
A company registered under the Companies Act,
Having its registered office at: MG Road, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Director Priya Menon.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
A limited liability partnership,
Having its office at: Whitefield, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Designated Partner Vikram Singh.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT UNDER SECTION 73 OF THE INDIAN CONTRACT ACT, 1872

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

COMMERCIAL COURT MAINTAINABILITY

1. The subject matter of the present suit arises out of a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. The suit is valued above the specified value of {{AMOUNT}} as prescribed under Section 12 of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and is therefore maintainable before this Hon'ble Commercial Court.

JURISDICTION

2. This Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 CPC, as the agreement was entered into at {{PLACE_OF_CONTRACT}}, was to be performed at {{PLACE_OF_PERFORMANCE}}, and the breach occurred at {{PLACE_OF_BREACH}}; in any event, the Defendant resides or carries on business within the local limits of this Court at Bengaluru.

3. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court. This Hon'ble Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain commercial disputes under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

LIMITATION

4. The suit is within limitation under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date on which the contract was broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

SECTION 12A COMPLIANCE

5. {{MEDIATION_STATUS -- CONFIRM: (a) Was pre-institution mediation conducted? If yes, provide mediation authority, date, and outcome certificate. (b) If not, is urgent interim relief being sought? Section 12A compliance is mandatory for commercial suits unless urgent interim relief is sought.}}

FACTS OF THE CASE

6. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into a written Software Development Agreement for the development of an ERP system, with a total contract value of Rs. 45,00,000/-. A copy of the said Agreement is annexed hereto as **Annexure A**.
7. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement, the Plaintiff paid a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- as the first milestone payment to the Defendant on 01.02.2024 via NEFT, bearing UTR No. ICIC20240201456789. A copy of the NEFT receipt is annexed hereto as **Annexure B**.
8. As per the express terms of the Agreement, the Defendant was obligated to deliver the first module of the ERP system on or before 01.05.2024.
9. On 01.07.2024, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice to the Defendant regarding the status of the project and the dues payable. A copy of the said legal notice is annexed hereto as **Annexure E**.

BREACH OF CONTRACT AND PARTICULARS

10. The Defendant failed to deliver the first module of the ERP system by the agreed deadline of 01.05.2024.
11. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant communicated its abandonment of the project via email, thereby repudiating the contract. Copies of the email correspondence evidencing abandonment are annexed hereto as **Annexure C**.
12. Despite the abandonment of the project, the Defendant has failed and refused to refund the advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- received from the Plaintiff.
13. The Defendant failed to respond to the legal notice dated 01.07.2024 sent by the Plaintiff, thereby confirming its refusal to honour its contractual obligations.

LOSS AND DAMAGE

14. The Plaintiff is entitled to the refund of the advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- paid to the Defendant, which remains unjustly retained by the Defendant.
15. Due to the non-delivery of the ERP system and the subsequent delay in operations, the Plaintiff suffered consequential losses amounting to Rs. 12,00,000/-, as assessed by an independent auditor. A copy of the loss assessment report is annexed hereto as **Annexure D**.
16. The Plaintiff took steps to mitigate the loss by issuing the legal notice and commissioning the audit report to quantify the damages accurately.
17. The Plaintiff claims total damages of Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rs. 20,00,000/- as refund of advance and Rs. 12,00,000/- as consequential damages), along with interest at 18% per annum from the date of breach until realization.

LEGAL BASIS

18. The Defendant has committed a repudiatory breach by refusing to perform its obligations, entitling the Plaintiff to treat the contract as at an end and seek appropriate relief.

19. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides that when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused thereby, which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach, or which the parties knew, when they made the contract, to be likely to result from the breach of it.

CAUSE OF ACTION

20. The cause of action first arose on 01.05.2024 when the Defendant failed to deliver the first module by the agreed deadline, thereby causing the Plaintiff's right to sue for damages to accrue.

21. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's claim for damages subsists and remains enforceable in law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

22. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to {{COURT_FEE_COMPUTATION_BASIS}} and the applicable rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree directing the Defendant to pay to the Plaintiff a sum of Rs. {{DAMAGES_AMOUNT}}/- as damages for breach of contract;
    (b) Award pendente lite interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from the date of breach / institution of suit till decree under Order XX Rule 11 CPC;
    (c) Award future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit from the date of decree till realisation under Section 34 CPC;
    (d) Award costs of the suit including advocate's fees to the Plaintiff;
    (e) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper;

SCHEDULE OF DAMAGES / PARTICULARS OF DAMAGES

The Plaintiff claims the following heads of damages with computation methodology:

(i) Actual and direct financial loss
     Amount claimed: Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/-
     Basis / methodology: {{ACTUAL_LOSS_BASIS}}
     Documentary proof: {{ACTUAL_LOSS_PROOF}}

TOTAL DAMAGES CLAIMED: Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/-

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Software Development Agreement dated 01.02.2024

Annexure B — NEFT receipt (UTR: ICIC20240201456789)

Annexure C — Email correspondence showing abandonment

Annexure D — Loss assessment report by auditor

Annexure E — Legal notice dated 01.07.2024

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 22 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect
